content.thinking:
Now I have all the information I need. I need to:

1. Generate a new node certificate (valid ~1 year, 365 days) using the CA in the toolbox
2. Generate a new client.root certificate (valid ~1 year) using the CA in the toolbox
3. Create the `crdb-cluster-certs` secret with: ca.crt, node.crt, node.key, client.root.crt, client.root.key
4. Restart the CockroachDB pods to pick up the new certs
5. Verify the changes took effect

The node.cnf already has the correct SANs, so I'll generate the new certificates with 365 days validity and assemble them into the secret.

content.text: Both node and client certs expire in 2 days. I'll generate fresh 1-year certs in the toolbox, then create the secret.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
# Generate new node key and CSR using existing config
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/node.key.new 2048 2>&1 && echo 'node key OK' &&
openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/node.key.new -out /tmp/certs/node.csr.new -config /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1 && echo 'node CSR OK' &&
# Sign for 365 days
openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/certs/node.csr.new -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key -CAserial /tmp/certs/ca.srl \
  -out /tmp/certs/node.crt.new -days 365 \
  -extensions v3_req -extfile /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1 && echo 'node cert signed OK'
"
input.description: Generate new node certificate valid for 365 days

content.content:
node key OK
node CSR OK
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN=node
node cert signed OK

tool_use_result.stdout:
node key OK
node CSR OK
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN=node
node cert signed OK

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
# Generate client root cert config
cat > /tmp/certs/client.root.cnf << 'EOF'
[req]
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
req_extensions = v3_req
prompt = no
[req_distinguished_name]
CN = root
[v3_req]
keyUsage = critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = clientAuth
EOF

# Generate new client root key and CSR
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new 2048 2>&1 && echo 'client key OK' &&
openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new -out /tmp/certs/client.root.csr.new -config /tmp/certs/client.root.cnf 2>&1 && echo 'client CSR OK' &&
openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/certs/client.root.csr.new -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key -CAserial /tmp/certs/ca.srl \
  -out /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new -days 365 \
  -extensions v3_req -extfile /tmp/certs/client.root.cnf 2>&1 && echo 'client cert signed OK'
"
input.description: Generate new client root certificate valid for 365 days

echo '=== New client cert validity ===' &&
openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new -noout -dates -subject
"

content.thinking:
The new certificates are valid for 1 year (until June 23, 2027). Now I need to:
1. Extract the cert/key data from the toolbox
2. Create the `crdb-cluster-certs` Kubernetes secret

I'll extract the base64-encoded data from the pod and create the secret.

content.text: Certificates are valid for 1 year. Now I'll extract them and create the Kubernetes secret.

input.command:
CA_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "cat /tmp/certs/ca.crt | base64 -w 0")
NODE_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "cat /tmp/certs/node.crt.new | base64 -w 0")
NODE_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "cat /tmp/certs/node.key.new | base64 -w 0")
CLIENT_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "cat /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new | base64 -w 0")
CLIENT_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "cat /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new | base64 -w 0")

kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-cluster-certs \
  --from-literal="ca.crt=$(echo $CA_CRT | base64 -d)" \
  --from-literal="node.crt=$(echo $NODE_CRT | base64 -d)" \
  --from-literal="node.key=$(echo $NODE_KEY | base64 -d)" \
  --from-literal="client.root.crt=$(echo $CLIENT_CRT | base64 -d)" \
  --from-literal="client.root.key=$(echo $CLIENT_KEY | base64 -d)" 2>&1
input.description: Create crdb-cluster-certs secret with new certificates

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data}' | python3 -c "
import json, sys, base64, subprocess
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
for k in data:
    print(f'  {k}: {len(data[k])} chars')
" 2>&1
input.description: Verify secret was created with all required keys

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods --no-headers 2>&1 | grep crdb-cluster | grep -v openssl)
  echo "[$i] $STATUS"
  READY=$(echo "$STATUS" | grep -c "1/1" || true)
  if [ "$READY" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods ready!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll until all 3 CockroachDB pods are ready
